evidence_reg: If the controller does not take action on the request of the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject without delay and at the latest within one month of receipt of the request of the reasons for not taking action and on the possibility of lodging a complaint with a supervisory authority and seeking a judicial remedy.
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA refers generally to notification of data breaches, while the REG concerns informing a data subject about inaction on a request; they do not clearly express the same constraint, so no quote-supported mismatch can be established.

evidence_reg: Where, in cases referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article, the controller is able to demonstrate that it is not in a position to identify the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject accordingly, if possible.
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA concerns data breach notification, whereas the REG concerns informing a data subject when identification is not possible; the texts do not clearly align or directly conflict.

evidence_reg: Where the controller has made the personal data public and is obliged pursuant to paragraph 1 to erase the personal data, the controller, taking account of available technology and the cost of implementation, shall take reasonable steps, including technical measures, to inform controllers which are processing the personal data that the data subject has requested the erasure by such controllers of any links to, or copy or replication of, those personal data.
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA mentions notification of data breaches, but the REG requires informing other controllers about an erasure request after public disclosure; there is no clear same-constraint or direct conflict comparison.
